skill.name: search-pubmed
skill.description: An intelligent tool for precision medical literature search using PubMed's E-utilities API.
skill.summary: Search PubMed for medical literature and retrieve article summaries using the official NCBI API.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 33 static findings were evaluated as false positives. The skill makes legitimate HTTP requests to NCBI's official E-utilities API endpoints (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) for PubMed search. Findings in SKILL.md related to shell backtick execution and cryptographic algorithms are false positives from markdown formatting. The JSON audit data file contains no executable code. The skill has a single clear intent: query PubMed and return article summaries. No evidence of malware, data exfiltration, command injection, or prompt injection was found.

low_findings.title: Python HTTP library usage for legitimate API call
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged urllib.request usage as a network concern. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The script uses Python standard library urllib.request to call the official NCBI E-utilities API (eutils.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov) for PubMed searches. This is the standard, authorized way to query PubMed programmatically. No data exfiltration or unauthorized network activity is present.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs to legitimate NCBI API endpoints
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged hardcoded URLs as a network concern. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The URLs reference official NCBI E-utilities API endpoints (esearch.fcgi and esummary.fcgi) which are the documented and only supported way to search PubMed programmatically.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive in data iteration
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged iteration over API response data as system reconnaissance. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The code iterates over PubMed article IDs returned from the NCBI API to extract titles. This is standard data processing for a search tool.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positive in JSON data file
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged content in search-pubmed_audit_result_v2.json as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The file is a data-only JSON file (audit results), not executable code. Any detected patterns are coincidental data content within the file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive in JSON data file
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged content in search-pubmed_audit_result_v2.json as system reconnaissance. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The file is a data-only JSON file containing audit results, not executable code.

low_findings.title: Markdown backtick formatting misidentified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md as Ruby/shell backtick execution. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The backtick characters are markdown formatting syntax for inline code and code blocks in documentation. SKILL.md is a documentation-only markdown file with no executable shell commands.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a GitHub URL in SKILL.md as a hardcoded URL network concern. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The URL is a source attribution link pointing to the skill's own GitHub repository for documentation purposes.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm false positive in markdown text
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged text content in SKILL.md as a weak cryptographic algorithm. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. SKILL.md is a documentation-only markdown file containing descriptions, instructions, and guidelines. No cryptographic algorithms exist in the file.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance false positive in documentation text
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged documentation text in SKILL.md as system reconnaissance. This is a FALSE POSITIVE. The text describes output discipline standards for research reproducibility.

content.value_statement: Finding the right medical literature requires searching through millions of PubMed articles. This skill helps you search PubMed and retrieve article summaries directly from the NCBI database.
actual_capabilities: Searches PubMed using the official NCBI E-utilities API
Retrieves the total count of articles matching a search query
Fetches article titles from search results
Supports standard PubMed search syntax including Boolean operators
Returns structured JSON output for programmatic processing
Includes retry logic for reliable API communication
limitations: Limited to PubMed database only, does not search other medical databases
Requires internet access to reach NCBI API endpoints
Returns a maximum of 5 article results per query
Does not retrieve full-text articles, only metadata and titles

use_cases.title: Systematic Literature Review
use_cases.description: A researcher conducting a systematic review can search PubMed for relevant articles and get title summaries for initial screening.

use_cases.title: Evidence-Based Clinical Decisions
use_cases.description: A healthcare professional can quickly search for the latest evidence on a treatment or condition to inform clinical decisions.

use_cases.title: Academic Writing Support
use_cases.description: A student writing a research paper can search PubMed to find relevant citations and article summaries for their literature review section.

prompt_templates.title: Basic PubMed Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for recent articles about diabetes treatment.
prompt_templates.scenario: New user trying PubMed search for the first time

prompt_templates.title: Specific Query Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for articles about the relationship between gut microbiome and autoimmune diseases.
prompt_templates.scenario: User with a specific research topic

prompt_templates.title: Combined Keyword Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed for clinical trials on immunotherapy for lung cancer published in the last 5 years.
prompt_templates.scenario: User filtering by article type and topic

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Boolean Search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search PubMed using the query: (COVID-19 OR SARS-CoV-2) AND (vaccine efficacy OR immunogenicity) AND clinical trial.
prompt_templates.scenario: Experienced user running complex Boolean queries

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for recent articles about hypertension management guidelines.
output_examples.output: Found 2,847 articles matching your query. Top titles include studies on updated hypertension treatment protocols, blood pressure target recommendations, and lifestyle intervention strategies.

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for links between sleep duration and cardiovascular disease.
output_examples.output: Found 1,523 articles matching your query. Key titles cover sleep duration effects on heart health, cardiovascular risk assessment, and circadian rhythm impacts on cardiac outcomes.

output_examples.input: Search PubMed for meta-analyses on ketogenic diet for epilepsy treatment.
output_examples.output: Found 312 meta-analyses matching your query. Results include studies on ketogenic diet efficacy in pediatric epilepsy, long-term outcome comparisons, and dietary modification protocols.

best_practices: Use specific and precise search terms to get the most relevant results from PubMed.
Combine terms with Boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT) for more targeted searches.
Verify the search returned the expected number of results before reviewing individual article titles.
anti_patterns: Do not use vague single-word queries as they return too many irrelevant results.
Do not rely on this tool for searches that require full-text article access or PDF downloads.
Do not use this tool to make clinical decisions without verifying results against the original published articles.

faq.question: What database does this skill search?
faq.answer: This skill searches the PubMed database maintained by the National Center for Biotechnology Information (NCBI).

faq.question: How many results can I get per search?
faq.answer: The skill returns up to 5 article results per search query along with the total count of matching articles.

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: No, this skill uses the public NCBI E-utilities API which does not require an API key for basic usage.

faq.question: Can I search for full-text articles?
faq.answer: No, this skill searches article metadata and titles only. It does not retrieve full-text article content.

faq.question: What search syntax is supported?
faq.answer: PubMed supports Boolean operators (AND, OR, NOT), exact phrase matching, field tags, and other standard PubMed query syntax.

faq.question: Does this tool work without internet access?
faq.answer: No, this tool requires internet access to connect to the NCBI E-utilities API endpoints.
